1.11a: Address: 7 Harbour Buildings, The Waterfront, Brierley Hill, DY5 1LN
1.11b: Address: 7 Harbour Buildings, The Waterfront, Brierley Hill, DY5 1LN
1.7: Lending is not permitted when the borrower and mortgagor are not the same person.
1.14: Legal firms may not act if the conveyancer or an immediate family member is the seller.
1.15: Legal firms may not act if the conveyancer or an immediate family member is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's name and address is required only upon specific request.
3.1.4: The lender must be notified if the seller is unrepresented or if representative checks are unsatisfactory.
3.1.5: Acceptable identity verification documents are listed under the Savings tab on the Dudley Building Society website.
3.2.3: Notification of seller's solicitor is only required if specifically requested.
3.2.4: The lender must be notified if the seller is unrepresented or if representative checks are unsatisfactory.
4.5a: Contact point for re-inspection is the Principal Office in Brierley Hill.
4.5b: Certificate of Title should be emailed to MortgageCompletions@dudleybuildingsociety.co.uk
4.1: Property inspection is not always mandatory for remortgages.
Solicitors should obtain a copy of the valuation report from the borrower if available.
Conveyancers must perform checks as per sections 4.2 and 4.3 regardless of the source of the valuation report.
4.3: Contact point for incorrect valuation assumptions is the Principal Office in Brierley Hill.
5.1.1: Contact Principal Office for cases where the seller has owned the property for less than 6 months.
Required info: previous vendor name/address, previous sale price, connections between parties, details of property work, and remortgage reason.
Cases must be put on hold until instructions are received.
Lending is prohibited for sub-sales, back-to-back transactions, or assigned sale contracts.
5.2.1: Contact Principal Office and confirm the identity of the seller if they are not the registered proprietor.
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports should not be sent.
Advise the lender of contamination entries found in local authority searches.
5.4.5: Personal searches are accepted subject to compliance with section 5.4.7.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted if it provides adequate protection and enables certification of marketable title.
5.5.3a: Report breaches of planning or building conditions to the Principal Office.
5.5.3b: Conveyancer must provide valid full planning permission.
Confirmation of submission of working drawings for Building Regulation Design Approval is required.
5.5.3c: Conveyancer must provide valid full planning permission.
Confirmation of submission of working drawings for Building Regulation Design Approval is required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development are not required to be sent but must be retained by the borrower.
The solicitor must notify the Society of any identified issues.
5.5.4: Restrictions affecting value or marketability must be reported to the Principal Office.
5.7.1a: Flying freeholds are acceptable if they affect no more than 25% of the property.
Requires existing cross covenants and a deed of mutual enforceability of covenants.
5.7.1b: Freehold flats are generally not acceptable.
Exceptions: Modern coach houses or single freehold properties where the applicant owns the entire block and other units are leasehold.
5.7.1c: Report acceptable freehold flats or flying freeholds to the Principal Office.
5.8.1: Security comprising a building with up to four flats is acceptable if the borrower occupies one and owns the freehold, with other flats on long leases.
5.8.5: Arrangements involving two leasehold flats where owners cross-own freeholds are acceptable.
Requires a charge over the borrower's leasehold interest and their freehold interest in the other flat.
5.10.1: Restrictions on use must be reported to the Principal Office.
5.13.1: Contact the Principal Office if the borrower is not providing the full purchase balance or proposes a second charge.
5.14.15a: Report absentee or insolvent landlords to the Principal Office.
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absent or insolvent landlords if it covers all lease requirements.
5.14.1: Minimum unexpired lease term for Capital & Interest: 85 years at drawdown.
Minimum term for Interest Only/Part & Part (LTV <= 50%): 85 years at drawdown.
Minimum term for Interest Only/Part & Part (LTV > 50%): 85 years remaining at end of mortgage term.
5.14.9: Lease-related matters should be reported to the Principal Office.
5.14.10: Service charge matters should be reported to the Principal Office.
5.14.11: Contact the Society regarding indemnity insurance for unsatisfactory lease terms.
5.14.12: Ground rent or service charge receipts are not required to be sent.
5.14.13: Evidence of service of notice is required.
5.15.2a: Report management company issues to the Principal Office.
5.15.2b: Management company share certificates do not need to be sent.
5.15.2c: Signed blank stock transfer forms must be sent after completion.
5.15.2d: Management company memorandum and articles of association do not need to be sent.
5.16.2: Contact the Principal Office if unable to certify that a search entry is irrelevant.
5.17.5a: A certified copy of the Power of Attorney must be sent after completion if used to execute the mortgage deed.
5.17.5b: A statutory declaration of non-revocation for a power of attorney is required.
5.19.1: Contact the Principal Office for matters regarding affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership.
5.20.1: Report solar PV panel roof space leases that do not meet UK Finance minimum requirements.
5.20.3: Solar PV panel leases must meet minimum UK Finance/CML requirements.
5.20.4: Details of existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed to the lender.
6.1.3: Notify the Principal Office if the borrower is not proceeding with the mortgage offer.
6.2.1: Report property description discrepancies to the Principal Office.
6.3.1: Report purchase price issues to the Principal Office.
6.4.5: Contact the Principal Office if the solicitor does not have control over all purchase funds.
6.5.1: Issues with vacant possession must be emailed to MortgageCompletions@dudleybuildingsociety.co.uk
6.6.1: Contact the Principal Office regarding properties to be let or buy-to-let status.
6.6.2: Contact the Principal Office if letting details are missing at completion.
6.6.3: Tenancy agreements do not need to be sent.
6.6.4: Lending on Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMO) is not permitted.
6.7.1: Acceptable warranty schemes: ABC+, Advantage (AHCI), Ark, Checkmate/Castle 10, LABC, NHBC, Premier Guarantee, Q, Protek, ICW, Self-Build Zone.
Global Home Warranties Limited and Buildzone are accepted for new builds only.
6.7.2: Send a certified copy of the warranty policy and certificate.
6.7.3: Building standards indemnity scheme assignments should be retained by the borrower; notify the lender only of issues.
6.7.4: Lending without a warranty is permitted if a Professional Consultants certificate is provided.
6.7.6: Professional consultant's certificates must be sent.
6.8.1: Report lack of agreements or bonds for unadopted roads/sewers to the Principal Office.
6.9.1: Report missing necessary easements to the Principal Office.
6.10.2: Retentions or advance instalments are released directly to borrowers.
6.11.1: Report redevelopment or road proposals to the Principal Office.
6.12.1: Report pre-emption rights, resale restrictions, or options to the Principal Office.
6.13.1: Report undischarged improvement or repair grants to the Principal Office.
7.3: Consent to mortgage is required from all occupants aged 17 or older.
7.4: Report doubts regarding information accuracy to the Principal Office.
8.1: Advice may be provided to specified third parties if no conflict of interest exists.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be sent after completion.
9.2: Indemnity insurance limit must be the higher of the purchase price or valuation.
10.2a: The mortgage advance is paid electronically.
10.2b: Minimum notice for electronic advance: 3 clear working days.
10.3: No standard deductions are made from the mortgage advance.
10.7: Returns of the advance due to delay (over 5 days) must be made via the same method received.
10.9: Contact the Principal Office if completion is delayed.
10.10: Advance may be held for a maximum of 5 working days.
If held, funds must be in an interest-bearing account with interest passed to the borrower.
10.11: Interest is charged from the date of the advance.
Advances must be returned immediately if completion is delayed more than 5 days or does not proceed.
12.3.1: Contact the Principal Office for release of retentions or instalments.
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is required for staged loan advances.
14.1.5: Certified copies of the mortgage deed and title documents are acceptable instead of originals.
14.2.1: Title deeds and documents must be uploaded electronically to LMS.
14.2.2: Post-completion documents for LMS upload: Priority Notice, Confirmation of Application to Register, Official Copy of Register, signed/dated Mortgage Deed.
If registration takes longer than 30 days, provide updates via LMS STARS every 30 days.
16.1.1: Contact the Principal Office for title document matters.
16.3.7a: Contact the Principal Office regarding execution of transfer of equity.
16.3.7b: Attestation clause: 'The Common Seal of Dudley Building Society was affixed in the presence of:'
16.3.1: The lender does not have a standard form for transfer or deed of covenant.
16.3.2: Contact the Principal Office for current debt amounts.
16.3.4: Transfers of equity require lender approval and are processed by redeeming the existing mortgage for a new regulated one.
16.4.1: Consent to letting applications email: Mortgages@dudleybuildingsociety.co.uk
16.4.2: A copy of the proposed tenancy agreement must be sent.
16.5.2: Contact for deed/agreement approval email: Mortgages@dudleybuildingsociety.co.uk
17.2.1a: Discharge and repayment remittance email: Mortgages@dudleybuildingsociety.co.uk
17.2.1b: Discharges are processed directly with the Land Registry.
